title: Continuum Parity Anomaly Is Not Lattice No-Go
summary: This conflict record preserves the inferential boundary between the continuum parity-anomaly / domain-wall story and the separate lattice Nielsen-Ninomiya theorem: the former motivates edge consistency, but it is not itself a proof of the lattice zero-sum law.
assumptions: Nearby sources and informal explanations often blur the continuum and lattice arguments.
regime: Scope-boundary preservation across related theorem families.
scope: Conflict-preservation object for anomaly intuition versus lattice proof.

source_anchors.notes: Witten uses anomaly inflow as boundary intuition, not as the lattice no-go proof.
The continuum edge-mode story remains distinct from the global lattice theorem.

representation: Conflict-preservation object keeping the continuum and lattice arguments distinct.
retrieval_hints: Use when an answer must explicitly preserve the difference between anomaly intuition and lattice proof.
